Vision", "EU cohesion and regional resilience", "EU cohesion/resilience", "EU internal cohesion and regional resilience", "Embedded Everything", "automation limits / infrastructure risk in modern banking", "cross-industry finance", "embedded finance", "embedded finance/payments", "embedded financial models beyond checkout", "execution", "execution gaps in European banking", "finance integration", "financial ecosystem", "fintech", "hybrid digital\u2013human banking experiences", "legacy infrastructure", "legacy infrastructure / legacy systems", "partnerships", "payments", "programmable capital markets", "strategic alignment"]
agenda_summary: Unchain Festival Unchain unites the Central and Eastern Europe as the growth market of Europe and a Global center of tech development. It provides a nexus for the financial industry and financial technology providers to come together. This year’s UNCHAIN Festival is positioned less as a “fintech event” and more as a meeting point for the entire financial ecosystem, where execution—not ideas—is the central theme. It brings together senior decision-makers (often C-level) from banking, fintech, payments, insurance, retail, and technology, alongside regulators, central banks, and EU institutions, reflecting how finance is now deeply cross-industry. The core message is that European finance has moved beyond innovation hype into a phase where legacy systems, AI adoption, and infrastructure decisions are make-or-break issues. Because finance is becoming embedded across sectors, the audience is no longer just banks—merchants, tech providers, and even non-financial companies entering financial services are increasingly relevant participants. The event is designed for people who need strategic alignment, partnerships, and practical execution insights, rather than early-stage inspiration or purely technical deep dives. In practical terms: if you’re a decision-maker shaping products, infrastructure, regulation, or partnerships in finance (or finance-adjacent industries), this event is for you; if you’re looking for hands-on coding, early startup pitching, or niche tech discussions, it’s likely not. Overall, UNCHAIN 2026 is best understood as a high-level, cross-industry strategy forum for those actively building or reshaping financial ecosystems in Europe. English is the official language of the event, ensuring clear communication for diverse Central and Eastern European audience.
